serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of steamed minced pork with mushroom is approximately 150g, equivalent to one medium-sized pork patty with mushroom pieces as served in a Singaporean home meal or hawker setting.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed Minced Pork with Mushroom — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Steamed Minced Pork with Mushroom (F06100042). All core macros and secondary nutrients are exact matches from this canonical Singapore food composition record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on low-carbohydrate mixed pork dish
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for steamed minced pork with mushroom. GI estimated at approximately 30 (low) based on analogous low-carbohydrate mixed meat dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database. Carbohydrate content is very low at 1.93g/100g, predominantly from mushroom and minor starch; the dish is protein- and fat-dominant. Glycemic load per 150g serving = (30 × 1.93 × 1.5) / 100 = 0.87, rounded to 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 493mg per 100g exceeds the 400mg/100g threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100g or less per serving and request reduced soy sauce during preparation, as sodium at 493mg/100g contributes approximately 25% of the 2000mg daily sodium target in a single 150g serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (1.93g/100g) and low sugar (1.18g/100g) with high protein (13.5g/100g) and dietary fibre (2.05g/100g) support glycaemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate and sugar content; however, the high fat content (16.46g/100g) should be moderated by choosing leaner pork cuts to reduce the risk of insulin resistance with frequent consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 5.8g/100g and sodium 493mg/100g are the primary cardiovascular risk factors in this dish
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish in moderation (100–120g portions), select lean minced pork with less than 10% fat, and reduce soy sauce to lower both saturated fat and sodium intake, which together elevate LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork contains arachidonic acid (omega-6) that amplifies inflammatory pathways; sodium at 493mg/100g may worsen RA-related tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting minced chicken or turkey for pork to reduce arachidonic acid and saturated fat load, and should reduce soy sauce to lower sodium, which may exacerbate joint inflammation and tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Minced Pork with Mushroom (香菇蒸肉饼, Xiānggū zhēng ròubǐng) is a classic Singaporean Chinese home-cooked dish consisting of seasoned minced pork mixed with rehydrated dried Chinese shiitake mushrooms, formed into a patty or loose mince, and steamed until cooked through. The dish is typically seasoned with soy sauce, sesame oil, and a small amount of cornstarch to bind the mixture, producing a tender, moist texture with a savoury umami flavour profile derived from both the pork and the mushrooms. It is commonly served over steamed white rice as part of a multi-dish family meal and is a staple in Singaporean Chinese households and hawker centres. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides 210 kcal, 13.5g protein, 16.46g fat (approximately 5.8g saturated), 1.93g carbohydrate, 1.18g sugar, 2.05g dietary fibre, 493mg sodium, 31mg calcium, and 1.3mg iron. The dish is protein-dense and very low in carbohydrates, making it suitable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes, but its elevated sodium content (493mg/100g) makes it a concern for individuals with hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The mushroom component contributes beta-glucans with modest anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties. Clinically, portion control and reduced soy sauce preparation are recommended for sodium-sensitive individuals.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F06100042), which is the canonical primary source for Singapore food composition data. The food name, description, and macronutrient profile are exact matches to the HPB record. Confidence is set to high.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific steamed minced pork with mushroom preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F06100042 as of 2026. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 5.8g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central reference for cooked ground pork (SR Legacy #10972) as a secondary cross-reference. Potassium estimated at approximately 280mg/100g based on USDA FoodData Central cooked ground pork reference. GI is an analogous estimate (approximately 30, low) as no direct GI measurement exists for this dish; based on low-carbohydrate mixed pork dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database. Input dietary_tags included 'vegetarian' which is incorrect for a pork dish and has been removed. Input occasion_tags included only 'breakfast' which is atypical; meal context updated to reflect standard lunch/dinner usage. Dish is not halal as it contains pork.
